articles.title: Outdated payment systems cost global businesses $330bn yearly, Airwallex finds
articles.summary: Airwallex and Cebr quantified a $330bn 'Global Growth Tariff' from legacy B2B cross-border payment inefficiencies, with EMEA businesses bearing roughly $144bn of that burden. The research supports Airwallex's policy and commercial positioning around modernising cross-border payment infrastructure.

articles.title: Airwallex acquires Leapfin to expand financial lifecycle capabilities
articles.summary: Airwallex acquired Leapfin to automate revenue recognition and reconciliation for global finance teams, deepening its product stack beyond core payments. Continued M&A expands the regulatory and data-handling surface area that product counsel must integrate.

articles.title: House of Lords: UK lagging on stablecoin regulation, ease BoE limits
articles.summary: The Lords Financial Services Regulation Committee's report 'Stablecoins: waiting for regulation' urged the BoE and FCA to reconsider holding limits, unremunerated backing-asset rules, and restrictions on banks issuing stablecoins. With the FCA's full crypto regime due 25 October 2027, the calibration directly affects payment firms planning GBP stablecoin or settlement products.

articles.title: FCA publishes responses on AML rules for cryptoasset firms
articles.summary: On 3 June 2026 the FCA published responses clarifying the interaction between the Money Laundering Regulations 2017 and cryptoasset firm obligations, including transfer-of-funds information requirements. Relevant for any Airwallex digital-asset or stablecoin-adjacent payment flows touching the UK.

articles.title: Revolut's planned US bank to offer stablecoins and FDIC-insured accounts
articles.summary: Revolut confirmed plans to offer stablecoin services and FDIC-insured accounts via a future US bank after filing for an OCC national bank charter in March 2026. The competitor's charter-led US strategy mirrors the broader fintech push for direct federal banking access that Airwallex must benchmark against.

articles.title: ASIC reporting & audit update confirms penalties for late lodgement
articles.summary: ASIC's 29 May update confirmed infringement notices to Canva and Mecca entities for late financial report lodgement, while outgoing chair Joe Longo flagged a depleting Enforcement Special Account. Overseas groups with Australian subsidiaries, including payment firms, face heightened reporting-compliance scrutiny.

articles.title: ASIC digital-asset 'no-action' relief deadline looms 30 June 2026
articles.summary: Providers of financial services involving digital-asset products must determine whether they need an AFS licence or variation and apply by 30 June 2026, when ASIC's sector-wide no-action position ends. A new Digital Assets Framework Bill will add DAP and TCP authorisations from April 2027, relevant to any Airwallex token-touching activity in Australia.

articles.title: MAS revokes Major Payment Institution licence of Bsquared Technology
articles.summary: MAS revoked BSQ's MPI licence effective 14 May 2026 after an inspection found serious breaches including risk-management and outsourcing failures and false or misleading information provided to the regulator. A clear signal of MAS's enforcement posture on licensing integrity for payment institutions in Singapore.

articles.title: FCA safeguarding supplementary regime now in force from 7 May 2026
articles.summary: The FCA's CASS 15 supplementary safeguarding regime took effect 7 May 2026, imposing daily reconciliation, monthly returns, annual audits and resolution-pack requirements on payment and e-money institutions. New guidance clarifies that standalone FX carried out independently of payment services need not be safeguarded, a material point for Airwallex's UK entities.

articles.title: FATF endorses revised cross-border payments standard at June plenary
articles.summary: FATF reached consensus on a revised payments standard that explicitly recognises non-bank financial institutions and promotes transparency in cross-border payments, building on the 2025 Recommendation 16 overhaul effective by 2030. This directly shapes the AML and data obligations Airwallex must build into ISO 20022-aligned payment messaging.
